title: Autographed Dani George Jersey
description: Dani George is an elite BMX racer who has competed internationally and represented Supercross BMX, a performance-focused BMX racing brand founded by Bill Ryan. Riders on the Supercross team compete in major BMX racing events worldwide, helping represent the continued growth of elite women’s BMX racing.

title: Harry Leary “Leary” “81” Redman Jersey
description: Redman BMX was a BMX racing brand active during the late 1970s and early 1980s. The company produced frames and racing apparel and was associated with riders such as Harry Leary, who competed on the Redman team during the early years of his career. Redman represents the era when small independent manufacturers helped shape the early development of organized BMX racing.

title: Connor Fields Signed Factory Misprint Jersey
description: This factory Answer BMX team jersey worn by Olympic champion Connor Fields is signed by the rider and carries a unique connection to BMX legend Harry Leary. The jersey was sent to Leary after he recognized it during a giveaway of items from Fields’ personal collection. The piece also features a rare factory misprint, with Fields’ name incorrectly spelled “Conner Fields,” making it a distinctive artifact linking two generations of BMX racing history.

title: Leary Thrill ROC 1 Jersey
description: This Thrill ROC #1 jersey was worn by BMX legend Harry Leary during his racing career. Jerseys like this represent the factory team identity and rider branding that helped define BMX racing culture during the sport’s early years.

title: Greg Hill GHP Jersey
description: This GHP team jersey represents BMX champion Greg Hill and his brand Greg Hill Products (GHP). Hill was one of the most dominant BMX racers of the 1980s, winning multiple national titles and later founding GHP to produce BMX racing frames and equipment. Jerseys like this reflect the transition of elite riders into brand builders within the BMX industry.

title: Harry Leary “Leary” BIOLAB ROC 1 Jersey
teams_brands_organizations: BIOLAB Sciences; Peak Performance; Race of Champions
description: This BIOLAB ROC #1 jersey was worn by BMX pioneer Harry Leary during his racing career. Sponsored by BIOLAB/PEAK PERFORMANCE, jerseys like this represent the factory team sponsorships that became a key part of BMX racing during the sport’s early competitive years.

title: Greg Mathias Signed Team USA Jersey
teams_brands_organizations: Team USA; USA BMX; JMC Racing
description: This Team USA jersey signed by Greg Mathias features a design inspired by the classic JMC Racing style associated with Mathias during his BMX racing career. Mathias was one of the top BMX racers of the early 1980s, and memorabilia like this jersey reflects his contributions to the sport and the enduring influence of the JMC brand in BMX racing history.

title: Leary Fasthouse “4” Jersey
description: This Fasthouse jersey represents BMX pioneer Harry Leary and reflects the continued recognition of Leary’s legacy within BMX culture. Produced by Fasthouse, jerseys like this celebrate the influence of early BMX legends whose riding helped shape the sport’s development.

title: GHP Jersey
description: This GHP team jersey represents the BMX racing brand Greg Hill Products, founded by BMX champion Greg Hill. GHP became known for producing high-performance BMX racing frames and equipment, continuing Hill’s influence on the sport following his dominant racing career in the 1980s.

title: John Stancliff Jersey
description: This BMX racing jersey belonged to local rider John Stancliff, representing the grassroots level of BMX competition that has always been the foundation of the sport. While professional racers often receive the most recognition, local riders like Stancliff played an important role in sustaining BMX racing communities through weekly track events and regional competitions. Artifacts such as this jersey help document the everyday racers who formed the backbone of BMX culture at local tracks.

title: JAG Jersey
description: The live Jersey Collection identifies this accession as a JAG jersey purchased from the BMX Museum. The current inventory CSV assigns 26.0035 to a JAG kit with an Oakley Factory Pilot decal. This public wall record follows the current Jersey Collection display while preserving the metadata conflict for resolution.

title: Harry Leary DIRTWERX Jersey
description: An authentic, race-worn Factory Team Jersey produced by DIRTWERX for BMX legend Harry Leary. This jersey features the classic heavy-duty ventilation and padded elbows characteristic of the Pro-Circuit era. As a companion piece to the DIRTWERX helmet and Leary's signature frames, this garment is a primary artifact of 80s/90s racing 'style' and functional design.

title: ACE Jersey — Given to Harry Leary in Australia
description: A rare piece of international BMX history: an original ACE (Australian Cycling Enterprises) jersey presented to Harry Leary during his competitive tours in Australia. This garment signifies the global expansion of the sport and the cross-continental influence of U.S. Factory Pros. A vital 'Survivor' piece that documents Harry's status as a global ambassador for the sport.

title: Peak “Leary” Jersey
description: A rare co-branded artifact from the peak of Harry Leary's career: the Peak Performance x BIOLAB factory jersey. This piece documents a pivotal era where BMX moved into the realm of high-performance athletics, with BIOLAB serving as the sport's early leader in rider conditioning and sports science. Featuring the iconic neon-heavy sublimation of Peak and the distinctive BIOLAB 'laboratory' branding.

title: BIOLAB “Leary” “4” Zeronine Jersey
description: This BIOLAB “Leary 4” jersey produced by Zeronine Apparel was worn by BMX legend Harry Leary during his later racing career. The jersey features Leary’s name and the number 4, reflecting his race number while competing with BIOLAB Sciences sponsorship. This artifact pairs with item 26.0076 in the Lititz BMX Archive and is documented in photographs of Leary wearing the jersey during competition and promotional appearances.

title: Harry Leary Memorial Jersey
description: This Harry Leary memorial jersey was produced for a gathering held to celebrate the life and legacy of BMX pioneer Harry Leary at Dirtyfest 3. The jerseys were created by fellow BMX racer Greg Mathias for the event, which brought together riders and friends from across the BMX community to honor Leary’s lasting impact on the sport.

title: Fall Risk Racing “Leary” Jersey
description: This Fall Risk Racing “Leary” jersey was gifted by BMX racer Greg Mathias in gratitude to Lititz BMX after the archive presented him with the 2017 Team USA “Learry” jersey discovered within the Leary Locker collection. The exchange reflects the strong sense of community and mutual respect within BMX, where riders and historians often work together to preserve and share the sport’s history.

title: Fall Risk Racing “Mathias” Jersey
description: This Fall Risk Racing “Mathias” jersey was gifted by BMX racer Greg Mathias to Lititz BMX in gratitude after the archive presented him with the 2017 Team USA “Learry” jersey discovered within the Leary Locker collection. The jersey reflects the camaraderie within the BMX community and the collaborative effort to preserve and share the sport’s history.

title: Specialized Jersey
description: This Specialized racing jersey represents the long-standing involvement of Specialized Bicycle Components in BMX and cycling culture. Jerseys like this were worn by riders and team members during races and promotional events, reflecting the brand identity and sponsorship presence that became a central part of competitive BMX.

title: Marzocchi Free Ride Team Jersey
description: This Free Ride Team jersey from Marzocchi represents the company’s involvement in freestyle and off-road cycling disciplines. Marzocchi became widely known for producing high-performance suspension forks used in BMX, mountain biking, and freeride competitions, and team apparel like this reflects the brand’s presence within action sports culture.

title: Auburn Jersey
description: This Auburn racing jersey represents the BMX brand Auburn Bicycles, known for producing high-performance BMX frames and equipment during the `908's and 1990s. Auburn played an important role in BMX racing during this period, supporting riders and contributing to the development of competitive BMX technology and team culture.

title: BIOLAB “Leary” “25” Jersey
description: This BIOLAB “Leary 25” jersey was worn by BMX pioneer Harry Leary during his later racing years. Sponsored by BIOLAB, the jersey reflects Leary’s continued involvement in competitive BMX decades after helping define the sport’s early racing era.

title: Harry Leary BIOLAB “25” Jersey
disposition: Gifted to a friend of Harry Leary
